flashy.services.logger package

Submodules

flashy.services.logger.emitter module

class flashy.services.logger.emitter.LogEmitter

Bases: QObject

Qt-based signal emitter for logging messages.

This QObject provides a thread-safe signal that can be used to transmit log messages from non-GUI threads to Qt GUI components.

Signals:
message_logged (str, int):

Emitted when a log message is produced. The first argument is the formatted log message, and the second argument is the logging level (e.g., logging.INFO, logging.ERROR).

message_logged
flashy.services.logger.emitter.emitter = <flashy.services.logger.emitter.LogEmitter(0x17f38ec8600)>

Global instance of LogEmitter.

This singleton emitter is used by logging handlers to broadcast log messages throughout the application, typically to GUI components.

flashy.services.logger.json_formatter module

Adapted from code by mCoding (https://www.youtube.com/@mCoding). Used with permission from the author.

Original source: https://github.com/mCodingLLC/VideosSampleCode/blob/master/videos/135_modern_logging/mylogger.py

Modifications by Jean-Emmanuel Chouinard:

  • Changed function name (myJSONFormatter –> JSONFormatter)

class flashy.services.logger.json_formatter.JSONFormatter(*, fmt_keys=None)

Bases: Formatter

format(record)

Format the specified record as text.

The record’s attribute dictionary is used as the operand to a string formatting operation which yields the returned string. Before formatting the dictionary, a couple of preparatory steps are carried out. The message attribute of the record is computed using LogRecord.getMessage(). If the formatting string uses the time (as determined by a call to usesTime(), formatTime() is called to format the event time. If there is exception information, it is formatted using formatException() and appended to the message.

Return type:

str

flashy.services.logger.logger_service module

flashy.services.logger.logger_service.setup_logging()

Configure application-wide logging using a JSON configuration file.

This function loads a logging configuration from src/flashy/services/logger/logger_config.json and applies it using logging.config.dictConfig().

The configuration defines loggers, handlers, formatters, and logging levels used throughout the application.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If the logging configuration file does not exist.

  • json.JSONDecodeError – If the configuration file contains invalid JSON.

  • ValueError – If the logging configuration is invalid.

flashy.services.logger.logger_service.get_logger()

Retrieve the main application logger instance named "flashy", which is defined in the logging configuration loaded by setup_logging().

Returns:

Configured application logger.

Return type:

logging.Logger

flashy.services.logger.qt_handler module

class flashy.services.logger.qt_handler.QtHandler

Bases: Handler

Logging handler that forwards log records to the Qt event system.

This handler formats log records and emits them through a Qt signal, allowing log messages to be displayed safely in the graphical user interface.

emit(record)

Emit a log record through the Qt logging signal.

Important

A new logging.LogRecord is created without exception information to not clutter the GUI log terminal.

Parameters:

record (logging.LogRecord) – Log record to emit.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None
